name: Julienned Leeks and Vegetables in Mustard Butter
recipeIngredient: 2 leeks, cleaned, slit, julienned (matchstick width and about 4 inches long)
recipeInstructions:
When cleaning leeks, remove withered outer leaves. Cut off the base and upper green leaves down to where the dark green begins to lighten. Slice in half and rinse gently under running water or in a pan of water, then rinse again.
Use a skillet with a good, transparent cover. Place broth in the skillet and bring to a boil. Add the largest vegetable parts first:
- Cook the upper carrot pieces for 4 minutes.
- Cook the white leek pieces for 2 minutes.
- Cook the thin asparagus stalks and remaining carrots for 1 minute each.
Remove the vegetables, drain well, and arrange in a covered shallow vegetable bowl to conserve heat. Add the mustard butter and toss. Cover to keep warm.
MUSTARD BUTTER: Cream the butter with Dijon mustard. Add lemon juice, dill weed, chives, white pepper, and salt.
